caption:
Here’s what the CIA has been up to for the last 80 years… PART 1

#fyp #mkultra #mockingbird #cia #epstein
audio_track_actual: Single male narrator, list/sprint format, walking through documented U.S. domestic intelligence programs roughly chronologically. Cuts away briefly at the end (transcript truncated to 180s).
video_overlay_text_observed: On-screen captions identifying each program by name as the narrator introduces it: PAPERCLIP, SHAMROCK, BLUEBIRD, ARTICHOKE, HTLINGUAL, MKNAOMI, MKULTRA, COINTELPRO, MKSEARCH, MKOFTEN, STARGATE, NORTHWOODS, GULF OF TONKIN.
audio_content_summary: The narrator runs through a chronological enumeration of declassified U.S. intelligence-community domestic and covert-action programs from 1945 through 1973, using each as a checklist of items already on the public record. Programs named: Paperclip (1945+), Shamrock (1945-1975), Bluebird (1950s), Artichoke (1950s), HTLINGUAL (mail surveillance), MKNAOMI (biological weapons), MKULTRA (1953-1973), MKSEARCH/MKOFTEN/MKCHICK and Stargate (1960s-70s remote-viewing programs), COINTELPRO (1956-1971, including the FBI 'suicide letter' to Dr. King), Operation Northwoods (1962), and the Gulf of Tonkin (1964). The narrator references the Unabomber's possible exposure to a Harvard psychological-experimentation program and Tom O'Neill's CHAOS book on possible MKULTRA connections to Charles Manson via Dr. Louis Jolyon West.
implied_frame: The video presents the breadth of declassified domestic and covert programs as a baseline of public-record fact, against which contemporary skepticism toward the intelligence community can be evaluated. The four programs newly documented under this entry — Paperclip, MKULTRA, COINTELPRO, and the Gulf of Tonkin — are each on the record via the Church Committee, NSA declassifications, or formal congressional acknowledgement. The downstream-research claims about Manson and the Unabomber are the editorial threads with the weakest documentary base; they are noted but not folded into the evidence cards.
capture_method: Headless browser recording of the in-page <video> element via MediaRecorder over captureStream(), viewport 1280x800.
